summary: Phase 1 expansion (Google auth, geo distance sort, city filter, ratings) fully tested. Backend: 37/37 pytest tests pass after I fixed one real bug in server.py geo-sort key (`d.get('distance_mi') or 9999` treated 0.0 as falsy, pushing Brickell Heights — the closest seeded venue — to the LAST position instead of FIRST when query coords matched its coords). Replaced with explicit None check. Now `GET /api/shifts?lat=25.7617&lng=-80.1918` returns shifts in true ascending distance order with Brickell first (distance=0.0). Verified: POST /api/auth/google returns 401 for invalid session_token (upstream Emergent OAuth returns 404, surfaces as 401 from our handler); GET /api/shifts/cities returns ['All Cities', 'Aventura', 'Doral', 'Miami', 'Miami Beach', 'Miami Gardens', ...] (sorted, leading 'All Cities'); GET /api/shifts?city=Miami filters correctly; all seeded shifts have lat/lng populated; ratings endpoint enforces all auth rules (officer must apply, ratee must equal posted_by, no duplicates, company can only rate own shifts' applicants); GET /api/users/{id}/ratings is public, returns count=0 + average=0 + empty list for nonexistent user. Frontend (mobile viewport 390x844): Welcome screen shows Google button + 'Insured & Bonded' chip + 'OR' divider; Login screen shows Google button; Browse screen has tappable location-selector-button that opens a bottom-sheet city modal with All Cities + sorted cities (radio button UI), selecting Miami refilters the list. Geolocation hook fires on mount (on web, browser denies which is OK). One UX nuance with Rate-this-Company: it conditionally renders only when officer has applied AND shift has posted_by — frontend already initializes `applied` from `s.applicants.includes(user.id)` at line 45 of shift/[id].tsx, so logic is correct. The pre-existing pointerEvents deprecation warning from a third-party RN dep still appears (unchanged from iteration 1, not app code).

minor.issue: FIXED by testing agent. Sort key `d.get('distance_mi') or 9999` pushed exact-match (distance=0.0) shifts to the end. Replaced with explicit None check. Verified Brickell Heights now sorts FIRST for Brickell coords.

react_native_issues.issue: Console warning 'props.pointerEvents is deprecated. Use style.pointerEvents' originates from a dependency, not app code. Not blocking.

action_items: (Optional) Now that ratings are wired, consider adding rating ingestion in shift card or browse list to show company stars without needing to open detail.
(Carried over from iteration 1) Investigate third-party props.pointerEvents deprecation source.

Main agent self-verify: tap on a freshly-applied shift (within the same session after Apply Now) and confirm 'Rate this Company' star UI + 'Submit Rating' button render and POST /api/ratings succeeds with 'Thanks for your rating!' toast.
critical_code_review_comments: server.py /api/shifts geo-sort had a subtle falsy-bug (0.0 treated as missing). Fixed.
/api/auth/google currently maps upstream errors to 401 — adequate for now, but a 502 path exists (httpx.HTTPError) and should be documented for API consumers.

Rating creation does not set a unique compound index until first ratings insert; the index `ratings (shift_id, rater_id, ratee_id) unique` is created on startup which is good. Consider adding `db.ratings.create_index('rater_id')` too for fast lookups in future profile views.
updated_files: /app/backend/tests/test_flexofficers_api.py (added 15 new tests: TestGoogleAuth, TestCities, TestGeoSort, TestRatings)

success_rate.frontend: 100% smoke (Google buttons, city modal, geo-request, navigation all OK)

seed_data_creation: Each test run creates 2 unique TEST_-prefixed users (officer+company), 2-3 TEST_-prefixed shifts (TEST_Patrol, TEST_RatingShift, TEST_Co2Shift, TEST_NoApplyShift), and 2 ratings (1 officer→company, 1 company→officer). No cleanup needed (prefixed for identification).
context_for_next_testing_agent: Backend URL: EXPO_PUBLIC_BACKEND_URL in /app/frontend/.env. Phase 1 testIDs added: welcome-google-button, login-google-button, location-selector-button, city-option-{cityName}, retry-location-button. Rate-this-Company section visible at line 163 of /app/frontend/app/shift/[id].tsx only when (user.role==='officer' && applied && shift.posted_by && !rated). The `applied` local state is initialized from `s.applicants.includes(user.id)` on load (line 45). When testing apply→rate flow, prefer creating fresh officer+company+shift via API setup rather than using seeded test_officer@example.com to avoid stale applicant state. POST /api/ratings creates a unique (shift_id,rater_id,ratee_id) index so duplicate ratings return 400. /api/users/{id}/ratings is intentionally PUBLIC (no auth). Geo-sort uses `d['distance_mi'] if not None else 9999` (post-fix). Backend default distance_mi for company-created shifts (without lat/lng) is 2.4.
rca_of_issue: Geo-sort bug RCA: `d.get('distance_mi') or 9999` uses Python truthiness — 0.0 is falsy in Python, so any shift whose computed distance happens to round to 0.0 (when query coords exactly match the venue coords, e.g. Brickell coords 25.7617,-80.1918 vs Brickell Heights 25.7616,-80.1917 → haversine ~ 0.01 mi → round(_,1)=0.0) gets sort key 9999 and ends up LAST. Reproduction: GET /api/shifts?lat=25.7617&lng=-80.1918 → before fix, output ended with 0.0 distance; after fix, 0.0 is first. Mitigation: replace `... or 9999` with `... if value is not None else 9999`.
